phases.name: Year 1
phases.subtitle: Explore
phases.description: Foundation in biological sciences and introduction to laboratory animal science career pathways.

milestones.label: Explore lab animal science careers
milestones.courseRef: LASC 162 — Intro to Animal & Lab Animal Sciences
milestones.description: Survey career pathways in laboratory animal science including pharmaceutical research, academic research facilities, and regulatory agencies. Students visit university and industry vivaria to understand the scope of the profession.

milestones.label: Complete orientation & academic planning
milestones.description: First-year advising and degree planning with emphasis on AALAS certification timeline, prerequisite sequencing, and co-curricular research opportunities.

milestones.label: Build biological science foundations
milestones.description: Foundational biology covering cell structure, genetics, ecology, and evolution. Laboratory component builds microscopy, experimental design, and data recording skills.

milestones.label: Study animal species & husbandry basics
milestones.description: Overview of domestic and laboratory animal species, breed characteristics, basic anatomy, and animal care principles. Hands-on experience at university animal facilities.

phases.name: Year 2
phases.subtitle: Engage
phases.description: Deepen scientific foundations through chemistry, microbiology, genetics, and medical terminology while engaging with professional communication.

milestones.label: Master agricultural genetics
milestones.description: Mendelian and population genetics applied to animal breeding and selection. Students analyze pedigree data and evaluate genetic models relevant to laboratory animal colony management.

milestones.label: Build medical terminology proficiency
milestones.description: Systematic study of medical and veterinary terminology. Essential for AALAS exam preparation, veterinary clinical communication, and research protocol documentation.

milestones.label: Complete microbiology training
milestones.description: Microbial diversity, pathogenesis, immunology, and laboratory methods. Students master aseptic technique, culture protocols, and diagnostic microbiology essential for vivarium health monitoring.

milestones.label: Develop professional communication
milestones.description: Oral communication skills for science presentations, IACUC protocol defense, and professional conference participation.

phases.name: Year 3
phases.subtitle: Develop
phases.description: Specialized coursework in laboratory animal biology, diseases, toxicology, and advanced anatomy builds clinical-grade expertise.

milestones.label: Master lab animal biology & diseases
milestones.courseRef: LASC 365 — Biology, Diseases & Care of Lab Animals
milestones.description: Comprehensive study of common laboratory animal species (rodents, rabbits, primates, zebrafish), their biology, husbandry requirements, common diseases, and veterinary care protocols. Central course for AALAS certification preparation.

milestones.label: Study toxicology principles
milestones.description: Study of toxic substances, dose-response relationships, and regulatory frameworks for preclinical safety testing. Students learn GLP compliance, study design, and the global regulatory landscape for pharmaceutical development.

milestones.label: Complete integrated anatomy
milestones.description: Detailed comparative anatomy with dissection-based laboratory. Systematic study of musculoskeletal, cardiovascular, and organ systems across species used in laboratory research.

milestones.label: Study animal sanitation & biosecurity
milestones.description: Infectious and parasitic diseases, biosecurity protocols, and sanitation practices for animal facilities. Focus on barrier systems, sentinel programs, and quarantine procedures in research environments.

phases.name: Year 4
phases.subtitle: Launch
phases.description: Clinical techniques, facility management, capstone seminar, and professional placement finalize AALAS certification readiness.

milestones.label: Master clinical techniques & management
milestones.courseRef: LASC 453 — Lab Animal Management & Clinical Techniques
milestones.description: Advanced clinical procedures including anesthesia, surgery, sample collection, and facility management. Students develop competencies in IACUC protocol compliance, colony health monitoring, and personnel training — the capstone clinical skills course.

milestones.label: Complete medical sciences training
milestones.description: Advanced study of medical science principles including pharmacology, pathology, and clinical diagnostics applied to laboratory animal medicine. Prepares students for veterinary school or advanced AALAS certification.

milestones.label: Present capstone seminar research
milestones.courseRef: LASC 489 — Seminar in Lab Animal Science
milestones.description: Capstone seminar requiring students to research, present, and defend a topic in laboratory animal science. Professional-grade oral and written communication assessed by faculty panel.

milestones.label: Complete professional internship
milestones.description: Supervised placement at a research vivarium, pharmaceutical company, contract research organization, or academic institution. Students demonstrate AALAS competencies in authentic professional settings.
